D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Primary Rainbow in a Raindrop

trace a sunbeam refracting, bouncing off the back wall, and exiting near forty two degrees

Every ray entering a raindrop refracts in, reflects once off the back, and refracts out. The exit angle is concentrated at a sharp caustic — the rainbow angle.

Sweep the impact parameter b/R to scan rays across the drop. The crimson exit ray traces the path; the gold dashed circle marks the 42° rainbow caustic. Use λ to dial wavelength via Cauchy dispersion (red shorter rainbow, violet larger).
